quoit: to play a throwing game similar to ringtoss [v QUOITED, QUOITING, QUOITS]
quonk: to make an accidental disturbing noise too close to a microphone [v -ED, -ING, -S]
quota: a proportional part or share [n QUOTAS]
quotative: a grammatical marker of a quotation [n QUOTATIVES]
quote: to repeat the words of [v QUOTED, QUOTES, QUOTING] : QUOTABILITY [n], QUOTABLE [adj], QUOTABLY [adv], QUOTATION [n], QUOTER [n]
quotha: used to express surprise or sarcasm [interj]
quotient: the number resulting from the division of one number by another [n QUOTIENTS]
quotition: a division regarded as a repeated subtraction [n -S]
qursh: a monetary unit of Saudi Arabia [n QURSHES]
quyte: (obsolete) to quit, also QUIGHT, QUITE [v QUYTED, QUYTING, QUYTES]
